description: Trajectoires long-terme : cardio capacity (VO2max, race predictions, fitness age, scores), zones recalibration (FCmax/LTHR/FTP boundaries), body composition (poids). Use case : review mensuel / fin de cycle pour valider l effet du plan.

panels.title: Hill Score (overall)
panels.description:
Numeric rating of your ability to run uphill, based on VO2 Max and training history.

Tiers: Recreational (1-24) | Challenger (25-49) | Trained (50-69) | Skilled (70-84) | Expert (85-94) | Elite (95-100).

Factors: Hill Endurance (sustain pace uphill, elevation gain + time on hills at low intensity), Hill Strength (maintain running power on hills, higher-intensity efforts), VO2 Max (peak oxygen consumption, predictor of uphill ability).

panels.title: FCmax (Running)
panels.description: Frequence cardiaque max utilisee par Garmin pour calculer les zones running. Recalibree apres test FCmax.

panels.title: LTHR (Running)
panels.description: Lactate Threshold Heart Rate utilise par Garmin. Recalibre apres test seuil 30min ou auto-detection durant les efforts.

panels.title: Resting HR
panels.description: Frequence cardiaque de repos utilisee pour calculer HRR (Heart Rate Reserve). Auto-update Garmin (moyenne sur sommeil).

panels.title: FTP Power (Running)
panels.description: Functional Threshold Power running. Auto-FTP calcule par la FR 955 a partir des efforts. Cible de progression : +20-40 W sur 6 mois de plan.

panels.title: Delta vs 30d
panels.description: Variation depuis ~30 jours.

targets.query: SELECT (last("weight") - first("weight")) / 1000 AS "delta" FROM "BodyComposition" WHERE time > now() - 30d

panels.title: VO2max
panels.description:
VO2max running, ranks LoL-style (percentiles Cooper + elite extrapolation):
Iron <28 (bottom 3%) | Bronze 28-37 (3-20%) | Silver 37-42 (20-42%) | Gold 42-47 (42-67%) | Platinum 47-53 (67-85%) | Emerald 53-60 (85-95%) | Diamond 60-68 (95-99.2%) | Master 68-75 (99.2-99.9%) | Grandmaster 75-80 (99.9-99.98%) | Challenger >=80 (top 0.02%, world class).

panels.title: Hill Score — Overall
panels.description:
Numeric rating of your ability to run uphill, based on VO2 Max and training history.

Tiers: Recreational (1-24) | Challenger (25-49) | Trained (50-69) | Skilled (70-84) | Expert (85-94) | Elite (95-100).

Factors: Hill Endurance (sustain pace uphill, elevation gain + time on hills at low intensity), Hill Strength (maintain running power on hills, higher-intensity efforts), VO2 Max (peak oxygen consumption, predictor of uphill ability).

panels.title: Hill Score — Strength
panels.description: Hill Strength measures your ability to maintain running power on hills. Based on higher-intensity hill efforts.

panels.title: Hill Score — Endurance
panels.description: Hill Endurance measures how well you can sustain pace and performance when running uphill. Based on elevation gain and time spent on hills with low intensity.

panels.title: Race Prediction — 5K

targets.query: SELECT mean("time5K") / 5 AS "Pace /km" FROM "RacePredictions" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.description: Garmin Race Prediction 5K. Tiers LoL-style bases table Daniels VDOT. Couleur = tier courant.

panels.title: Race Prediction — 10K

targets.query: SELECT mean("time10K") / 10 AS "Pace /km" FROM "RacePredictions" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.description: Garmin Race Prediction 10K. Tiers LoL-style bases table Daniels VDOT. Couleur = tier courant.

panels.title: Race Prediction — Half Marathon

targets.query: SELECT mean("timeHalfMarathon") / 21.0975 AS "Pace /km" FROM "RacePredictions" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.description: Garmin Race Prediction Half Marathon. Tiers LoL-style bases table Daniels VDOT. Couleur = tier courant.

panels.title: Race Prediction — Full Marathon

targets.query: SELECT mean("timeMarathon") / 42.195 AS "Pace /km" FROM "RacePredictions" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.description: Garmin Race Prediction Marathon. Tiers LoL-style bases table Daniels VDOT. Couleur = tier courant.

panels.title: HR Zone Boundaries — Trajectory
panels.description: Evolution des floors Z1..Z5 + LTHR + FCmax sur la duree du plan. Si Garmin recalibre une zone, tu verras un step ici.

targets.query: SELECT last("zone1Floor") AS "Z1", last("zone2Floor") AS "Z2", last("zone3Floor") AS "Z3", last("zone4Floor") AS "Z4", last("zone5Floor") AS "Z5", last("lactateThresholdHeartRate") AS "LTHR", last("maxHeartRate") AS "FCmax" FROM "HRZones" WHERE "sport"='RUNNING' AND $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(previous)

panels.title: Power Zone Boundaries — Trajectory
panels.description: Evolution des floors Z1..Z5 + FTP. Auto-FTP Garmin = pilote l'echelle des zones. Une bonne progression du plan = FTP qui monte progressivement.

targets.query: SELECT last("zone1Floor") AS "Z1", last("zone2Floor") AS "Z2", last("zone3Floor") AS "Z3", last("zone4Floor") AS "Z4", last("zone5Floor") AS "Z5", last("functionalThresholdPower") AS "FTP" FROM "PowerZones" WHERE "sport"='RUNNING' AND $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(previous)

panels.title: Threshold — LTHR (% FCmax · bpm · pace)
panels.description: Trajectoire LTHR (Lactate Threshold HR). Serie principale = LTHR / FCmax(historique) en %. FCmax historique : snapshot HRZones quand dispo (>= 2026-05-01), fallback sur la plus vieille valeur connue (195) pour les dates anterieures. Bandes : <82% debutant, 82-87 recreatif, 87-92 bien entraine, 92-95 elite endurance, >95 elite mondial. Survole pour voir LTHR (bpm), pace seuil, et FCmax utilise.

targets.query: SELECT mean("pct_fcmax") AS "% FCmax" FROM "ThresholdComputed" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null) tz('Europe/Paris')
SELECT mean("lthr_bpm") AS "LTHR (bpm)" FROM "ThresholdComputed" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null) tz('Europe/Paris')
SELECT mean("pace_per_km_sec") AS "Pace seuil" FROM "ThresholdComputed" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null) tz('Europe/Paris')
SELECT mean("fcmax_used") AS "FCmax bpm" FROM "ThresholdComputed" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null) tz('Europe/Paris')
SELECT mean("weight") / 1000 AS "kg" FROM "BodyComposition" WHERE $timeFilter GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Cardiac Efficiency (Power/HR) — 14d Trend
panels.description: Ratio mean(Power) / mean(HR) par jour (running, samples Power>100W et HR>130bpm pour exclure warmup/cooldown). Plus le ratio monte, plus tu produis de watts par bpm. Tendance ascendante sur 90j = adaptation aerobie reussie.

targets.query: SELECT mean("Power") / mean("HeartRate") AS "W/bpm" FROM "ActivityGPS" WHERE $timeFilter AND "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "Power" > 100 AND "HeartRate" > 130 GROUP BY time(1d) fill(null)

panels.title: Eddington — Running (Lifetime)

targets.query: SELECT "distance" FROM "ActivitySummary" WHERE "ActivitySelector" =~ /running/ AND "distance" > 1000 AND time > '2010-01-01T00:00:00Z' LIMIT 10000

panels.description:
Distribution Eddington - runs >= N km pour chaque N de 10 a max(runs_km). Filtre N>=10 pour zoomer sur la zone de crossing (l'Eddington actuel est dans cette zone).

CE QUE ÇA MONTRE
Barres bleues : nombre de runs >= N km. Ligne grise pointillee : seuil de qualification y=x (= N).

EDDINGTON VISUEL
L'Eddington E = derniere barre qui depasse encore la ligne diagonale. Pour qu'un E=N soit valide, il faut barre(N) >= N (= diagonale). Des que barre(N) tombe sous la ligne, on a depasse E.

NEXT LEDGE
A N=E+1, la barre est juste sous la diagonale. Le 'gap' visuel (diag - barre) = nombre de runs manquants pour passer a E+1.
Exemple : si barre(14) = 13 et diag(14) = 14, il manque 1 run de >= 14 km pour passer a E=14.

LECTURE LONG-TERME
Avec des LR systematiques, les barres autour de 14-18 km grossissent. L'Eddington progresse a mesure que les bars depassent la diagonale.

PIPELINE
1. Query distances lifetime
2. Conversion km
3. Histogram bucketSize=1 (= 1 bar par km entier)
4. Tri descending par bucket
5. Cumulative sum sur la colonne count (cumsum desc = count >= N)
6. Tri ascending pour affichage
7. Field diagonal = BucketMin (pour tracer y=x)
8. Filter BucketMin >= 1

FILTRE
Sorties running uniquement (=~ /running/), distance min 1 km, lifetime depuis 2010.
Heat acclimation percentage from Garmin. >75% = well acclimated. Seasonal pattern expected.
panels.title: Heat Acclimation

panels.description: Altitude acclimation from Garmin. Shows the altitude (in meters) you are fully adapted to. Tracking activates above 800m. Range: 800-4000m.
panels.title: Altitude Acclimation

title: 08 Long-Term Trends
